title: Douglasia ochotensis
taxonomy: Douglasia ochotensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). It belongs to the genus Douglasia and is native to Russia and the United States. The classification of Douglasia ochotensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. This classification helps to group plants based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Douglasia ochotensis is a type of alpine plant that grows in mountainous regions.
uses: Douglasia ochotensis has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, although its efficacy and safety for human consumption have not been extensively studied or verified by modern scientific research. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial for treating various ailments. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal applications. In traditional folk medicine, Douglasia ochotensis has been used to treat fever, wounds, and other conditions. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.

constituents: Douglasia ochotensis, also known as Ochotense or mountain douglasia, is a plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. The plant Douglasia ochotensis contains iridoid glycosides, which are known for their medicinal properties. These active constituents are responsible for the plant's potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Research on the plant's active constituents is ongoing, and more studies are needed to fully understand their effects on human health. parts: Douglasia ochotensis. The roots and rhizomes of Douglasia ochotensis are used in traditional medicine, primarily by the indigenous people of the Russian Far East and northeastern Asia. intro: Douglasia ochotensis, also known as Ochotensis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae), native to the mountainous regions of eastern Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Douglasia ochotensis include iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicroside, and other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Douglasia ochotensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and infusions.
